content.problemStatement:
A post on facebook is said to be more popular if the number of likes on the post is **strictly greater** than the number of likes on some other post. In case the number of likes is same, the post having more comments is more popular.

Given arrays AAA and BBB, each having size NNN, such that the number of likes and comments on the ithi^{th}ith post are AiA_iAi‚Äã and BiB_iBi‚Äã respectively, find out which post is most popular.

It is guaranteed that the number of comments on all the posts is **distinct**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of posts.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚Äî where AiA_iAi‚Äã is the number of likes on the ithi^{th}ith post.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã ‚Äî where BiB_iBi‚Äã is the number of comments on the ithi^{th}ith post.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, an integer in the range 111 to NNN, denoting the index of the post which is most popular among the NNN posts.
constraints: The elements of array BBB are distinct.
It is guaranteed that the sum of NNN over all test case does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The number of likes on the first post is greater than that of second and third post. Thus, the first post is most popular.
Test case 222: The first and second post have maximum number of likes. But, the second post has more comments than the first post. Thus, the second post is most popular.
Test case 333: The number of likes on the third post is greater than that of first and second post. Thus, the third post is most popular.
Test case 444: The number of likes on the second post is greater than that of first, third, and fourth post. Thus, the second post is most popular.
